summary: This cluster investigates the detection and quantification of harmful algal bloom (HAB) species, specifically focusing on the ichthyotoxic raphidophyte *Chattonella marina* complex, using advanced molecular methods. The research employed droplet digital PCR (ddPCR) to quantify this species from marine plankton samples, revealing a range of internal transcribed spacer (ITS) copy numbers from 25 to 112 per cell with no significant variation between spiked environmental DNA samples. The findings demonstrate that ddPCR offers a precise alternative to traditional microscopy for monitoring problematic marine protists, supporting management strategies for HABs across diverse geographic and ecological marine environments.
